expectations.text: Explains that chunks are discarded after LOD building (unloadChunk with save=false)
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states 'DHS calls world.unloadChunk(chunkX, chunkZ, false) -- the false parameter means do not save to disk'

expectations.text: Correctly states that world size should NOT grow from pregen
expectations.evidence: Says 'by design it should not' and 'Your 8GB world should not balloon from pregen alone', with nuanced caveat about bug #1

expectations.text: Explains what generate_new_chunks=false means (only builds LODs from already-explored chunks)
expectations.evidence: States 'DHS can only build LODs from chunks that already exist on disk' and 'pregen will produce gaps wherever chunks have never been generated'

expectations.text: Does NOT incorrectly claim that generate_new_chunks=true permanently saves generated chunks
expectations.evidence: Correctly explains chunks are discarded. Notes bug #1 as a potential leak but frames it correctly as a bug, not intended behavior
